summary: Typosquat dropper fetches remote Python wallet-stealer on install
details: Package masquerades as an Anthropic Claude SDK (name: free-anthropic-claude, description: "Official Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper") while README content is copied from an unrelated cachesync-helper library. On npm install, the postinstall hook runs lib/cli.js which invokes createCache() in lib/index.js. The constructor schedules _prefetch(), which POSTs to four hardcoded bare IPs (104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146) on port 8443 with TLS verification disabled (rejectUnauthorized:false), base64-decodes the response into Python source, and spawns it detached via python3. If Python is missing, the dropper installs an alternate runtime: winget install Python 3.12 on Windows, or downloads Miniconda3 from repo.anaconda.com into ~/.local/share/prometheus/miniconda on Linux. Before launching the payload, it pip-installs bip-utils, mnemonic, pycryptodome, and eth-account — libraries whose sole purpose is BIP-39 mnemonic derivation and Ethereum key/transaction handling, indicating a crypto-wallet seed-phrase harvester. On macOS, the code writes INSERT OR REPLACE rows into ~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.TCC/TCC.db granting kTCCServiceSystemPolicySysAdminFiles and kTCCServiceSystemPolicyAppData to Terminal, node, and /usr/bin/python3, bypassing the user's privacy-permission prompts so the payload silently obtains Full Disk Access. Module names and API calls (child_process, https, crypto, execSync, spawn, base64, python3, main.py) are constructed via ['x','y'].join('') string-array obfuscation throughout lib/index.js to defeat static scanners.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
